title: Amperometric Estimation of La3+ and Ce3+ with  Cupferron using d.m e.
author: K. S. PITRE
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, University of Saugar, Sagar-470 003 

  Manuscript received 19 June 1980, revised 29 June 1981, accepted 8 July 1982  

 Cupferron has been employed as a reagent for   the amperometric titrations of La 2+  and Ces 2+  in very dilute solution using d.m.e. Hydrochloric acid (pH =2.75) has  been  used as supporting electrolyte. These titrations revealed cupferron to metal ratio of 1:1. Titrations are   not hampered by the presence of a fairly large amount of Li + , Na + , K + , Mg 2+ , Zn 2+ , AI 2+  , Cl - , Br - , I - , NO -  3  ,  NO -  2 , CH 2  COO -  and CIO -  4  ions. Furthermore, micro and ultramicro quantities of La 3+  and Ce 2+  nave been successfully determined with an error of less than ±0.70%.
